es.example: Poco a poco nos fuimos acostumbrando.
es.exampleTranslation: Little by little we got used to it.

it.example: A poco a poco ci siamo abituati.
it.exampleTranslation: Little by little we got used to it.

bridge.note: The same phrase, off by one particle: Italian doubles the a (A poco A poco), Spanish keeps only the middle one. A difference small enough to miss for years — which is precisely the kind this product exists to point at.

interference.es_to_it: Spanish drops the first a — importing it ('a poco a poco') marks you instantly, though everyone understands.
interference.it_to_es: Italians drop nothing and gain nothing: adding the first a to Spanish ('a poco a poco') just sounds Italian.

review.notes: From Joe's Italian teacher's prepositions crash course, 2026-07-16. ES pairing chosen by Claude, unverified — reviewer to confirm.
